title: Redash
description: Open-source data visualization and dashboarding tool to query, explore, and share insights from 30+ SQL/NoSQL/API data sources.
installation: Redash is a multi-process stack: every service runs the SAME redash/redash image with a different command. This template runs the primary web server (command `server`, port 5000). You MUST ALSO deploy these companion deployments from the identical redash/redash:10.1.0 image, same env vars, to make it functional: a Celery WORKER (`celery worker --app=redash.worker --queue=queries,celery`) for running queries, a SCHEDULER (`celery worker --app=redash.worker --beat --queue=periodic,emails,schemas`) for refreshes and alerts, and a one-time INIT/migration job (`create_db`) that must run ONCE before first boot to create the schema. Bundled add-ons Postgres and Redis (provided by the Valkey add-on) are provisioned here. ROTATE before exposing: REDASH_COOKIE_SECRET and REDASH_SECRET_KEY (each `openssl rand -hex 32`; SECRET_KEY must be 32+ chars and must NOT change after data exists or saved credentials become undecryptable), plus the Postgres add-on password. Set REDASH_HOST to your public URL. After the create_db job completes and the server is up, open the app and the first registration becomes the admin. Optional: outbound email needs REDASH_MAIL_* SMTP env vars (not provisioned).
